content:
# Alright, I need @everyone in this server to chime in.
<@605159327942049844> has brought up an important point that needs to be addressed, in my opinion *especially* in regards to gameplay text, that being the issue that we have Translators and Proofreaders working simultaneously in this project.

His concern is valid in that CN Translators need priority on sections of work *before* proofreaders get to it, but I believe if utilized well, my public repo's Ledger system can handle that issue.

# My main concern is this:
It's very possible that some languages' teams do not have a dedicated translator **FROM CN** into their language, which would have them default to translating **FROM EN**. The problem here is that a lot of the EN Gameplay text is... a little inaccurate at best, and iterating from it risks a wacky "game of telephone" where Function becomes CN becomes EN becomes {XX} and **meaning is lost each step of the way.**

Then comes the noted facts that (a) on the one hand, I intend to work on effectively a complete overhaul of the EN gameplay text to make it clean, standardized, and most importantly, *accurate;* and (b) on the other hand, BIAV has also stated a desire to redo all of the CN gameplay text themselves eventually, which may or may not include changing the functions of the game (as they did in JP server), which would make my work (and yours) outdated and in need of being redone.

We have a few ways to deal with this, assuming they *don't* intend to change effectively all of the gameplay text in the game:

- First, of course, we could **ignore the problem** entirely.
  - Every team would continue with what they're doing, and if your team isn't double-checking all of their work against the real functions of the game, your language's game experience may suffer.
  - When I finish with my overhaul would have effectively no difference on your team's translation capacity or its results.

- Second, we could **put faith in each team** to have a dedicated **CN or Function Translator**.
  - Every team would need to ensure anyone who's doing work on "Gameplay Function text" is fluent either in exactly how the game works in every case *(ideal)*, or in Chinese *(gets you close enough)*.
  - When I finish with my overhaul, the option would be available for every team to double-check their work against mine and make sure everything matches up and checks out.

- or Third, we could have every team **wait for me** to do at least a first pass on the Gameplay content.
  - This way we can make sure everything in English is worded correctly, and have Ace—if not implement it in the game yet—at least confirm that it looks good before having every other team iterate off of it.
  - Ace has stated BIAV is hesitant to let me do this much work for various reasons, mostly because they do have plans to hire dedicated English translators for the entire game, but that's a long ways away, and I'm eager and willing, so I was gonna do it anyway.
  - My reasoning there *was* that even if they don't use my overhaul directly, the structuring choices I make could still help inform the devs on how they want to move forward; but with every other language, Ace has stated that the game in that language would stay as the volunteers contributed it for the foreseeable future, which means my structuring reform could have a significant impact on the readability of any language that is iterating from EN.

Looks like the general consensus is that we should designate trusted Translators and have each team independently rely on them to translate before doing proofreading.
I've created a <@&1419930759182942309> role, and enabled the setting that **allows anyone to @ping them** to help facilitate this. 
## If you speak, read, or understand Chinese well enough to be confident enough to do a better job translating from CN into EN or your team's language, reply to this post and I'll give you the <@&1419930759182942309> role.

# Additionally, we should clarify the current status of each team and their ability to translate from CN.
If a team doesn't have the ability to confidently translate the original CN text, then they should wait for EN to become a more accurate translation, or at least be aware that they may want or need to redo their work later on.

- <@&1402540806539051120>
  - CN: Has confident translator(s) - BIAV, <@605159327942049844> 
  - Gameplay: Has confident translator(s) - <@183782216353054720>, <@364412673275723779>
- <@&1402540915477975163>
